summary: Iteration 2 testing: panels entity + tech specialty refactor + i18n. Backend: 30/30 pytest tests PASSED (auth, specialty-aware /api/questions filter, panels CRUD, electrical/mechanical RBAC on inspections, target/category mismatch 400, all filters target_type/category/target_number/technician_id/date_from/date_to, CSV w/ panel rows, stats.total_panels, user specialty validation). Frontend: end-to-end smoke ALL PASS - tech1(elec) sees only branch-elec-machines + branch-panels; tech3(mech) sees only branch-mech-machines + branch-chillers; back-button returns to menu; panel inspection submit returns to menu; admin shows all 7 tabs; panels tab CRUD; user-form has specialty-select when role=technician; inspections filter columns correct; lang toggle (TopBar+Login) flips html dir rtl↔ltr; 0 console errors.

issues: A few short toast/welcome labels still render in Arabic after English toggle (e.g. welcome toast on Panels tab shows mixed AR/EN). Not blocking; i18n catalog for toasts is incomplete.
Native browser date inputs (mm/dd/yyyy) used instead of a styled localized date picker — same minor visual gap noted in iteration 1.

action_items: (Optional) Translate the few remaining toast/inline labels (e.g. welcome toast) when language is English.
(Optional) Replace native date inputs in admin Inspections filter with a localized RTL/LTR-aware date picker.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py startup re-creates seed techs only when missing AND only patches specialty when present — this is correct but during testing the techs were absent until backend restart (CI environments may need a forced reseed/restart hook).

Inspection POST validates allowed_categories BEFORE target/category mismatch — current 403/400 ordering is correct (category-mismatch test now passes because electrical category IS allowed for tech1, and target_type=chiller correctly returns 400).

Route order: /inspections/{iid} is declared before /inspections/export/csv. FastAPI routing matched correctly in tests, but defining static path before parameterized is the safer convention (carryover from iteration 1).
CSV export endpoint queries db.inspections without restricting by user — only admin can call it via require_admin, so OK.
panels seed runs only if collection is empty — if a previous iteration removed all panels the seed will only re-run on full restart; consider per-document upsert if data drift becomes an issue.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login/specialty branches/back-button/inspection submit/admin 7 tabs/panels CRUD visible/specialty select/lang toggle)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests create+delete TEST_ prefixed users/machines/panels/inspections via fixtures. Backend was restarted once during this iteration to re-seed techs (iteration 1 had deleted them).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers 30 tests for new schema (category/target_type/target_id/target_number, specialty RBAC, panels CRUD, filters, CSV). Login uses data-testid=login-email-input/login-password-input/login-submit-button. Tech branch testids: branch-elec-machines, branch-panels, branch-mech-machines, branch-chillers. Admin tabs: admin-tab-{overview|inspections|machines|chillers|panels|questions|users}. Inspection form: back-button, target-select, q-{id}-yes/q-{id}-no, submit-inspection. Lang toggle: lang-toggle-login (login page) and lang-toggle (topbar). If backend has stale seed (techs missing), run: sudo supervisorctl restart backend && sleep 4.
